summary: Three new top-level tests plus one sub-tested helper test cover the behaviors introduced by Task 03. Each test asserts observable behavior rather than implementation. Litmus test: removing each new production line of code causes at least one assertion to fail. Test pyramid stays unit-heavy (all four are unit-level), consistent with the report package's existing style. One coverage gap noted (no end-to-end test that exercises the full readTar→processData→WARNING path on a tar with empty IO columns); deferred to Task 04 since that task explicitly regenerates golden tar fixtures and would naturally add this coverage.

findings.location: report/report_test.go (no end-to-end test for WARNING path)
findings.issue: Test_emitProcPidStatAvailabilityWarnings exercises the helper in isolation; Test_readMeta_with_sysinfo exercises the readTar→sysinfo path. No test wires both together by feeding a tar with two ticks of procpidstat data where cols 9-10 are empty strings, then asserts the WARNING line appears in app.writer before the first data row in the full processData output. Existing acceptance criterion is covered by the unit-level helper test; an integration-level test would catch wiring regressions where the helper is called from the wrong place in processData.
findings.recommendation: Defer to Task 04 — that task explicitly regenerates report golden fixtures (testdata/pgcenter.stat.golden.tar) and adds Test_app_doReport_procpidstat. A golden-file variant with empty IO sentinels naturally provides this coverage.

findings.issue: Test asserts items[1] but does not assert items[0].meta.ticks == 0 (first-tick lag). If a future change made readTar emit sysinfo eagerly into items[0], the test would still pass even though the documented one-tick-lag contract changed silently.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add 'assert.Equal(t, float64(0), items[0].meta.ticks)' alongside the items[1] assertion to make the one-tick-lag contract explicit and tested.

litmusChecks.production_line: report/report.go: sysinfo branch in readTar (json.Unmarshal into stat.SysInfo, meta.ticks/cpuCount assignment)

pyramidBalance.assessment: healthy — matches report package style; e2e coverage is in Task 04's scope
